basics.name: Alexis Chupin
basics.label: Full-Stack Engineer
basics.summary: French full-stack engineer with 5 years of software engineering experience across private banking, cybersecurity, and web development. Based in Bucharest, I build business-critical web platforms across React/TypeScript and Spring Boot/Java, with hands-on ownership of architecture, APIs, databases, CI/CD, production support, security remediation, and AI-assisted development using GitHub Copilot.

work.name: Extia
work.summary: Consulting mission for Société Générale Private Banking, building and operating an internal credit management platform.
highlights: Built and evolved a business-critical web application replacing complex Excel-based credit workflows for Private Banking teams in France.

Worked across the full product lifecycle: solution design, relational data modeling, front-end and back-end development, testing, CI/CD, production releases, monitoring, bug fixing, and functional evolution.
Designed multi-source API integrations to aggregate dossier, stakeholders, guarantees, and financial data into a single application.
Developed advanced business screens with real-time calculations, complex forms, safe navigation flows, and unsaved-changes protection.
Led major back-end and database evolutions, including Flyway migrations, production data scripts, feature flags, batch jobs, and APIs exposed to other internal systems.
Owned the application as sole developer during its latest phase, handling development, releases, production incidents, and service continuity.
Remediated pentest findings, including access-control and DTO update issues, by tightening back-end validation and update logic.
Used GitHub Copilot daily to accelerate implementation, technical exploration, and configuration work across React and Spring topics.
Delivered internal presentations on AI and developer use cases to senior stakeholders.

work.name: Thales
work.summary: Three-year apprenticeship contributing to security tools for the Galileo network.
highlights: Designed and delivered a navigation message generation tool for government Galileo messages, eliminating generation failures and reducing form completion time by 30%.
Built an offline graphical mapping tool for visualizing, creating, and manipulating 3D objects to support operators on cybersecurity software.
Provided support on cybersecurity software through bug fixes, feature additions, and additional test cases.

work.name: HommeDeMetier
work.summary: Internship focused on modernizing an internal website and building an e-commerce site with a forum.
highlights: Implemented an authentication system for administrators and account creation for new trainees.
Built a forum for article publishing with SEO-oriented forms, listing, filtering, and article display pages.
Contributed to the e-commerce site across back-end product retrieval and front-end pagination, filters, and payment flow.

education.area: Computer Science and Electronics (Major: Computer Science and Information Systems; Minor: Cybersecurity)
